synthesis_text: COVID-19 spreads from person to person through inhaling air contaminated by droplets/aerosols and small airborne particles containing the virus [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00024], particularly in close proximity, but also over longer distances indoors [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00004]. It can also be transmitted by touching eyes, nose, or mouth after touching contaminated surfaces [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00004].

COVID-19 spreads through the air in tiny droplets of fluid [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000], by breathing in droplets or particles from close people [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008], and from person to person, even from someone with no symptoms [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007].

COVID-19 spreads through small droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, speaks, sings or breathes [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00003], and also by touching mouth, nose or eyes after touching contaminated surfaces [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00005].
